skill.name: memory-init
skill.description: Initialize a memory system in the current directory, generating CLAUDE.md, MEMORY.md, and a memory/ directory for daily notes. Triggered when user says 'initialize memory', 'set up memory', 'memory init', or '/memory-init'.
skill.summary: Initialize a memory system in the current directory with CLAUDE.md, MEMORY.md, and memory/ directory.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate memory system initialization tool that generates configuration files for Claude and Codex. Backtick patterns are markdown code fences, not shell execution. Hidden file references are safety instructions to ignore system directories, not malicious access. No cryptographic code or obfuscation present.

medium_findings.title: Static Scanner Flagged Markdown Backticks as Shell Execution
medium_findings.description: The static scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at multiple lines, but these are markdown code fences (```), not actual shell commands. This skill is documentation that shows Claude how to use Read, Write, Glob, and Grep tools - it does not execute external commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All 'backtick' detections are markdown code fences, not Ruby shell operators. The skill is pure documentation with no executable code.

medium_findings.title: Static Scanner Flagged Home Directory References as Filesystem Access
medium_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 'hidden file in home directory' at lines 90 and 93 referencing ~/.claude/. However, the skill explicitly instructs Claude to IGNORE this directory, not to access it. This is a safety instruction, not a threat.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill says 'ignore ~/.claude/' - this is a directive to avoid a directory, not to access it. Confirmed by reading line 93: '忽略 ~/.claude/ 下的 auto-memory（内置记忆系统），不要读也不要写'.

content.user_title: Initialize Memory System for AI Assistants
content.value_statement: AI assistants lose context between conversations. This skill sets up a persistent memory system with CLAUDE.md, MEMORY.md, and daily memory files so Claude and Codex can maintain continuity across sessions.
actual_capabilities: Check existing memory files and prompt for overwrite or merge decisions
Scan project directory to auto-generate project summary for CLAUDE.md
Collect user persona information through interactive questions
Generate CLAUDE.md with custom persona and standardized memory protocol
Create MEMORY.md template for long-term memory storage
Set up memory/ directory for daily memory files
limitations: Does not automatically read existing memory files - users must start conversations with memory context
Memory files are plain text and require manual maintenance over time
Only initializes files in current working directory, not global or project-wide

use_cases.title: New Project Onboarding
use_cases.description: When starting a new project, run this skill to set up memory files that help Claude understand the project context, your role, and maintain memory across sessions.
use_cases.target_user: Developers starting new projects with Claude Code

use_cases.title: Personal AI Assistant Configuration
use_cases.description: Configure your preferred interaction style, work habits, and memory preferences so Claude provides more personalized assistance.
use_cases.target_user: Users who work extensively with AI assistants

use_cases.title: Multi-Project Memory Management
use_cases.description: Set up separate memory systems for different projects or clients, keeping contexts isolated and relevant.
use_cases.target_user: Freelancers and consultants managing multiple projects

prompt_templates.title: With Project Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Initialize memory for my current project. I am a backend developer working on a Python REST API.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you already know your project type and role

prompt_templates.title: Force Reset
prompt_templates.prompt: Reset my memory system. I want to start fresh with new settings.
prompt_templates.scenario: When existing memory files need to be replaced

prompt_templates.title: Check Memory Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my current memory files. What exists and what needs updating?
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to review existing memory before starting work

output_examples.output:
I found your project is a Python web scraper. I'll set up memory files with your developer persona.

Generated files:
- CLAUDE.md (persona + memory protocol)
- MEMORY.md (long-term memory template)
- memory/ (daily memory directory)

Next time we talk, I'll automatically load this context.

output_examples.input: Remember that I prefer concise responses and work on JavaScript projects
output_examples.output:
Saved to MEMORY.md:
- Response style: concise
- Primary language: JavaScript

output_examples.input: What should I remember from our conversation today?
output_examples.output:
Key takeaways from today:
- Discussed memory system setup
- Created CLAUDE.md with your preferences
- Project: Node.js Express API

Should I save these to memory/2026-03-20.md?

best_practices: Initialize memory at the start of each new project to establish clear context from day one
Review and update MEMORY.md monthly to keep personal preferences and project details current
Use the daily memory feature for session-specific notes that would clutter long-term memory
anti_patterns: Do not skip the persona questions - your responses help Claude provide more relevant assistance
Do not ignore memory files once created - regularly update them with project changes
Do not create memory files in multiple directories for the same project - this causes context fragmentation

faq.question: What is the difference between CLAUDE.md and MEMORY.md?
faq.answer: CLAUDE.md defines your persona, communication style, and the memory protocol. MEMORY.md stores long-term facts about you and your projects that persist across all conversations.

faq.question: Can I use this with both Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. During initialization, you can choose to generate both CLAUDE.md (for Claude Code) and AGENT.md (for Codex/ Cursor) with identical content.

faq.question: How often should I update my memory files?
faq.answer: Review MEMORY.md quarterly or when starting significantly different work. Update daily memory files at the end of each conversation for any important decisions or context.

faq.question: What happens if I already have existing memory files?
faq.answer: The skill will detect existing files and ask whether to overwrite them, merge content, or keep them unchanged. This protects your existing memory from accidental deletion.

faq.question: Will this interfere with Claude Code's built-in memory features?
faq.answer: The skill explicitly tells Claude to ignore built-in memory systems and use the project-local memory files instead. This gives you full control over what context is available.

faq.question: How do I use the daily memory feature?
faq.answer: At the end of each conversation, Claude will ask if anything is worth remembering. Say 'remember this' during conversation for instant saves, or use 'save to memory' for longer summaries.
